strengths: Fragment-only swap is the correct approach — reuses the existing vertex shader (noise3d + pointWarp.vert) unchanged, avoiding duplication of the complex deformation pipeline
Correctly identifies that uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, and uTime are already in the uniform block; only uVoronoiGridSize is truly new
Fog implementation matches the existing pointWarp.frag.glsl pattern exactly (smoothstep on vDepth, desaturation toward cool tint, 85% alpha cap)
Edge color uses RGB darkening of vColor rather than an HSL round-trip — correct since vColor is already palette-derived RGB from the vertex shader
Self-contained hash function (fract/sin/dot) avoids coupling to noise3d.glsl which is only prepended to the vertex shader string
Test suite covers both positive (uniform present, shader source content) and negative (voronoi disabled, no uVoronoiGridSize) paths, plus quality tier propagation
Step 0 bulk restore avoids fragile per-step file recovery

concerns.issue: enableVoronoiCells defaults to true for medium AND high tiers, making voronoi the default point cloud shader for most users. The ticket says 'alternative fragment shader' — consider defaulting to false everywhere and exposing it as an explicit opt-in (e.g. via mode manager config or a separate voronoi point cloud mode), or at least only enabling it for the high tier so medium remains familiar.
concerns.suggestion: Set enableVoronoiCells to true only for high tier, false for medium and low. This keeps the voronoi shader as a premium visual while still satisfying 'can be assigned to at least one geometry system'.

concerns.issue: The current import is `import fragmentShader from '../shaders/pointWarp.frag.glsl?raw'` as a top-level const. Step 3 should clarify that both fragment shaders are imported at the top level and selection happens in init(), not that the import itself is conditional (dynamic imports would complicate Vite bundling).
concerns.suggestion: Import both at top level: `import pointWarpFrag from '...pointWarp.frag.glsl?raw'` and `import voronoiFrag from '...voronoiCell.frag.glsl?raw'`, then select in init() via `const frag = useVoronoiShader ? voronoiFrag : pointWarpFrag`.

concerns.issue: draw() conditionally updating uVoronoiGridSize only in voronoi mode adds a branch every frame. Since Three.js silently ignores uniforms not declared in the active shader, the uniform could be set unconditionally.
concerns.suggestion: Always set uVoronoiGridSize in the uniforms object at init time and update it unconditionally in draw(). Three.js will ignore it when the standard fragment shader is active. Simplifies draw() logic.

concerns.issue: The 9-cell voronoi loop (nested -1..1 x/y iteration) runs 9 distance computations per fragment. On mobile low-end GPUs with large point sizes this could be costly, but the quality gating (disabled on low tier) mitigates this.
concerns.suggestion: No action needed — quality gating is sufficient. Could add a comment in the shader noting the O(9) cost for future maintainers.

riskAreas: Voronoi hash function quality — fract(sin(dot())) can produce visible patterns on some GPU architectures. If artifacts appear, may need to switch to a better hash (e.g., integer-based).

Point size interaction — voronoi cells only look good at sufficient gl_PointSize. Very small points (low structureComplexity, far depth) may degrade to noise rather than readable cells. May need a minimum point size threshold when voronoi is active.
